mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market tightness and wage-driven consumption growth, which drives immediate positive equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in both industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized broad-based demand expansion across goods and services sectors, which mechanically supports corporate earnings revisions and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in more than 60% of rolling 3-year windows, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising consumer price indices reflect pent-up demand normalization rather than supply constraints, allowing equity markets to price higher nominal earnings without discount rate penalties, yielding positive quarterly returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL prints consistently above 3.0% annualized while S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain negative in more than 50% of observations, confirming inflation-driven multiple compression dominates.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in retail sales volume directly increases near-term corporate revenue expectations for consumer-facing equities, driving immediate positive price discovery and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.05 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in federal funds rate hikes exceeding 0.5 percentage points over two months compresses equity valuation multiples and increases corporate borrowing costs, which mechanically depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The triggered instances produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of cases, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm structural labor market tightening, which sustains wage-driven consumption growth and supports positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive risk-adjusted return differential versus a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion phases, a sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.5 percent signals demand exhaustion and margin compression, which triggers immediate negative equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold trigger produces positive or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample instances, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a 10-year backtest.
